name: Vehicle Mount
description: An interaction that transfers a character into a vehicle seat, assigns driver or passenger authority, and validates safe exit when leaving.
design_notes: Vehicle mount is a state transfer, not only an interaction prompt. Define mount_interaction_id, seat_id, ownership, driver and passenger authority, animation sync, collision handoff, exit position rules, disconnect behavior, and anti-grief constraints. The mechanic should feel immediate while preventing seat theft, duplicate drivers, and unsafe exits.
design_purpose: Let players enter and leave vehicles through a readable, validated interaction.
Assign driver, passenger, camera, and input authority without desync or ownership exploits.
Connect traversal, combat, multiplayer, and vehicle systems through a safe state handoff.
player_fantasy: The player feels they can confidently board a vehicle, take control or ride along, and exit without losing agency.

parameters.name: mount_prompt_radius_units
parameters.description: Interaction radius around eligible vehicle seats.

parameters.name: mount_enter_window_ms
parameters.description: Committed entry interval before seat authority is granted.

parameters.name: seat_authority_policy_id
parameters.description: Policy that decides who can occupy and control each seat.

parameters.name: exit_position_rule
parameters.description: Rule used to choose a safe world position on exit.

parameters.name: mount_cancel_window_ms
parameters.description: Early cancellation window before collision and authority handoff commit.

parameters.name: disconnect_restore_policy_id
parameters.description: Behavior when a mounted player disconnects or host migration occurs.

edge_cases: Two players request the same seat in the same server tick and need deterministic tie-breaking.
The vehicle moves during the entry animation and changes the mount transform.
Exit positions can be blocked by walls, hazards, water, or enemy bodies.
Disconnect or host migration while mounted needs seat ownership and vehicle state restoration.
common_bugs: Client grants driver control before the server approves seat_id ownership.
Entry animation completes but collision handoff leaves the character capsule active inside the vehicle.
Exit validation places the player under the vehicle or inside a nearby wall.
Passenger camera remains attached after dismount because camera_transition_profile_id is not cleared.
balancing_notes: Long entry windows create vulnerability; instant entry improves usability but can enable escape exploits.
Seat ownership rules matter in co-op and PvP because players can grief by stealing driver seats.
Exit safety should protect players from geometry without allowing teleport shortcuts through locked spaces.
accessibility_notes: Use clear seat prompts with driver/passenger labels and remappable interaction input.
Avoid rapid camera snaps on mount or dismount; provide comfort settings for vehicle camera transitions.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent mount_interaction_id as a transaction: reserve seat_id, disable or park the character controller, transfer input_authority_id to the vehicle, and validate exit_position_rule before restoring the player capsule. Netcode should replicate seat occupancy from the server.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area prompts around seat anchors, then commit mount state through a vehicle_seat_system node. On exit, run shape queries for candidate positions before re-enabling the player collision body.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use possess or attach flows only after the server validates seat ownership. Replicate seat_id, driver_player_id, and passenger states; use animation montages for entry while collision and authority transfer remain gameplay-owned.
implementation_notes.web: Store vehicle_mount_state with vehicle_id, seat_id, player_id, authority_owner_id, and pending exit rule. Apply the seat transaction atomically in the simulation loop so clients cannot create duplicate drivers.

example_games.title: Halo: Combat Evolved
example_games.note: Uses driver and passenger vehicle seats as a combat traversal layer.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto V
example_games.note: Uses vehicle entry and exit interactions across many vehicle types.
